tickerText: [TENNIS] Wimbledon 2026 singles draw released Friday at 10 a.m. BST: Jannik Sinner tops the men's field as defending champion with Alexander Zverev at No. 2 after Carlos Alcaraz's wrist-injury withdrawal; Aryna Sabalenka is women's No. 1 seed with Iga Swiatek defending at No. 2; seven-time champion Novak Djokovic is seeded seventh; Serena Williams awaits her first-round opponent on wildcard return • [SQUASH] PSA Challenger circuit delivers fresh titles as the tour's summer break begins — French wildcard Benjamin Aubert wins the 15k Vendee Open in Challans with five straight upsets; Guatemala's Alejandro Enriquez lifts the 2nd Guatemala Open at home over top seed Ronal Palomino; Noah Meredith takes the Screwfix Brighton Open • [BADMINTON] YONEX US Open Super 300 quarterfinals day at Titan Gym in Fullerton: 18-year-old Rounak Chouhan stuns World No. 6 and top seed Chou Tien Chen 21-17, 26-24 for his first top-10 win and a quarterfinal berth; Kidambi Srikanth, Tanvi Sharma, and Treesa Jolly-Gayatri Gopichand advance through earlier rounds • [PICKLEBALL] Portland PPA Challenger opens Friday at The Picklr Westbrook in Maine with 244 players and a $10,000 prize pool across five pro divisions; Jack Wong Hong-kit's Beijing Open gold still resonating after his all-Arronax final win over Zane Ford at the National Tennis Center • [TABLE TENNIS] WTT United States Smash 2026 begins qualifying Friday at Ontario Convention Center outside Los Angeles — $1.55M prize pool, 11-day Grand Smash run through July 5; defending champions Wang Chuqin and Zhu Yuling return alongside Sun Yingsha, Tomokazu Harimoto, Felix Lebrun, Kanak Jha, and Lily Zhang

newsItems.title: Wimbledon 2026 Singles Draw Released as Serena Williams Awaits Opponent
newsItems.excerpt: The Wimbledon singles draw ceremony took place Friday, June 26, at 10 a.m. BST, setting the 128-player fields for both events. Defending champion Jannik Sinner is the men's No. 1 seed with Roland Garros winner Alexander Zverev at No. 2 after Carlos Alcaraz withdrew with a wrist injury. Aryna Sabalenka tops the women's draw with defending champion Iga Swiatek at No. 2, while seven-time champion Novak Djokovic is seeded seventh. Serena Williams, handed the final women's singles wildcard, will learn her first-round opponent as main-draw play begins Monday, June 29.

newsItems.title: Aubert and Enriquez Headline PSA Challenger Wins
newsItems.excerpt: With the 2025/26 PSA Squash Tour season closed after the Paris finals, the Challenger circuit delivered fresh titles. French wildcard Benjamin Aubert won the 15k Vendee Open in Challans with five consecutive upset victories, including a final win over Mohamed Sharaf for the biggest title of his career. Guatemala's Alejandro Enriquez captured the 2nd Guatemala Open at home, defeating Colombian top seed Ronal Palomino 11-3, 5-11, 11-4, 11-7 for his first Challenger silverware since 2024.

newsItems.title: Chouhan Stuns Top Seed Chou to Reach US Open Quarterfinals
newsItems.excerpt: Eighteen-year-old Rounak Chouhan produced the biggest win of his career at the YONEX US Open 2026, stunning World No. 6 and top seed Chou Tien Chen 21-17, 26-24 in the Round of 16 at Titan Gym in Fullerton, California. Chouhan rallied from 15-17 down in the opener and saved four game points while erasing a 17-11 deficit in the second to claim his first victory over a top-10 opponent. He advances to Friday's quarterfinals to face Israel's Misha Zilberman as the Super 300 event moves into its penultimate weekend.

newsItems.title: United States Smash Opens With $1.55M Grand Slam in Los Angeles
newsItems.excerpt: The WTT United States Smash 2026 begins Friday at the Ontario Convention Center in Greater Los Angeles, the second Grand Smash of the season with a $1.55 million prize pool. Qualifying kicks off across an 11-day run through July 5 as defending champions Wang Chuqin and Zhu Yuling return alongside Sun Yingsha, Tomokazu Harimoto, Felix Lebrun, and Hugo Calderano. Americans Kanak Jha, Lily Zhang, and Amy Wang join wild cards Nandan Naresh and Sally Moyland as the sport's elite converge in Southern California ahead of the LA28 Olympic cycle.
